name: Non-LLM Constructed-Response Augmentation Search
inclusion_criteria: Study uses generated, transformed, simulated, oversampled, or feature-space synthetic data derived from or representing student constructed responses.
Study concerns automatic scoring/grading or automated analysis of short answers, constructed responses, scientific models, or similarly open student work.
Synthetic data is used to address sparse, limited, or imbalanced training/evaluation data, or the paper explicitly studies that role.
Preference for non-LLM or pre-GPT examples, but later non-LLM/manual/feature-space examples may be included if they answer the same research-process question.
exclusion_criteria: Pure essay-scoring examples already represented in the sparse-data baseline unless needed for contrast.
Reference-answer augmentation without synthetic or transformed student-response data.
General NLP augmentation papers without an educational constructed-response or ASAG task.
Operational policy documents without extractable research-process role.
dedupe_method: Manual deduplication by title, DOI, and source URL.

screening_decisions.title: Multiple Data Augmentation Strategies for Improving Performance on Automatic Short Answer Scoring
screening_decisions.reason: Direct pre-GPT/non-LLM short-answer scoring augmentation example with full-text extraction.

screening_decisions.title: Improving the Performance of Automatic Short Answer Grading Using Transfer Learning and Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Direct ASAG text-augmentation example, but extraction is limited to official abstract/search-indexed ScienceDirect text.

screening_decisions.title: Cross-Prompt Based Automatic Short Answer Grading System
screening_decisions.reason: Direct short-answer grading example using manual response augmentation and SMOTE to address imbalanced classes.

screening_decisions.title: Utilizing Deep Learning AI to Analyze Scientific Models: Overcoming Challenges
screening_decisions.reason: Adjacent constructed-response/scientific-model example using SMOTE and image augmentation for imbalanced automated scoring categories.

screening_decisions.title: Paraphrase Generation and Supervised Learning for Improved Automatic Short Answer Grading
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant adjacent ASAG augmentation work, but the synthetic material is multiple correct reference answers rather than synthetic student responses.

screening_decisions.title: Improved Automated Essay Scoring Using Gaussian Multi-Class SMOTE for Dataset Sampling
screening_decisions.reason: Non-LLM synthetic sampling example, but this pass prioritized constructed-response and short-answer examples outside essay scoring.

screening_decisions.title: Modeling Short Answer Grading Performance Improvement by GPT Augmentation Data
screening_decisions.reason: Potentially relevant GPT short-answer augmentation, but the current pass targeted older/non-LLM examples.

screening_summary: Targeted search identified multiple non-essay examples of synthetic or augmented constructed-response data used to supplement sparse or imbalanced datasets. The strongest direct text-response example is Lun et al. 2020; Krisnawati et al. 2025 adds manual and feature-space augmentation; Li et al. 2025 is adjacent non-text constructed-response evidence; Bonthu et al. 2023 is direct ASAG evidence but abstract-only in this extraction.
limitations: This is a targeted bootstrap search, not a systematic review.
Bonthu et al. was not full-text extracted because direct publisher page retrieval was blocked.
Feature-space and image augmentation records should not be interpreted as generated textual student responses.
